metrics.narrative: DuBois manages $300-400M in combined direct and indirect spend with a 9-10 person central sourcing team (Tharunkumar Chandirasekaran, 3 Dec 2025 intro call), and pain is specific: Chris Klein described losing roughly 1,000 hours a year to manual contract data entry with minimal net benefit from prior tools (11 Mar 2026 CLM deep dive). However, no dollar-quantified ROI or business case has ever been built against the $300-400M spend base across more than seven months of engagement, and the detailed quote shared 30 Jan 2026 was never validated against a value case.

champion.narrative: Tharunkumar Chandirasekaran has been the consistent internal driver since 3 Dec 2025: he joined Showpad and reviewed all resources (29 Dec 2025), coordinated the legal team's participation in the 11 Mar 2026 CLM deep dive, and transparently disclosed internal restructuring and budget-gate changes on 26 May 2026. He is a Sourcing Analyst, not an executive, has not succeeded in brokering EB access after repeated attempts since March 2026, and the project's priority is now being set above his level by a new corporate governance team.

competition.narrative: Coupa is a deeply embedded incumbent handling invoicing and supplier onboarding; Tharun stated on 26 Feb 2026 'I don't think we'll be getting rid of Coupa,' and Zycus's own solutions consultant told the buyer that keeping supplier onboarding in Coupa might be 'the natural choice' rather than a third-party solution (26 Feb 2026) — a self-inflicted competitive opening. SAP Ariba is named in Salesforce and referenced as DuBois's existing e-bidding tool (3 Dec 2025) but was not raised as an active competing bid in any later call. A broader ~10-vendor shortlist was underway but never completed before the project was paused.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is well articulated and consistent across calls: fragmented, offline indirect-spend processes and manual supplier outreach (12 Dec 2025 discovery), a rudimentary, under-leveraged contract repository driving CLM interest (12 Dec 2025), missed rebate/payment milestones from manual Excel tracking (11 Mar 2026), and Chris Klein's explicit complaint that prior contract tools added work instead of saving it (11 Mar 2026). This is tempered by an explicit coexistence requirement: DuBois has no plans to replace Dynamics 365 or Coupa.

paper_process.narrative: No paper process has started. The deal remains in Formal Evaluation with the vendor shortlist incomplete; there is no MSA, SOW, or Order Form activity referenced in any call or Next Step entry, and commercial negotiation has not begun beyond an initial quote and a detailed follow-up quote (30 Jan 2026).

economic_buyer.narrative: Jackie Zhang (VP Procurement) is the named Decision Maker in Salesforce, and Next Step History shows repeated attempts to bring her into the conversation: a VP connect was tentatively planned for 24 Mar 2026, then end of April 2026, then a meeting was added for the last week of April 2026 (13 Apr 2026 note). None of these materialized: Jackie has not appeared as a speaker on any of the seven read calls between 3 Dec 2025 and 26 May 2026. Tharunkumar remains the sole conduit to her and has said he will represent the sourcing team but defers company-level decisions upward (26 May 2026 call).

decision_process.narrative: The original process was a ~10-vendor bake-off narrowing to a top-3 shortlist by end of Q1 2026, followed by group demos with Jackie, Ryan and legal, IT integration review, and a parallel C-suite budget push (26 Feb 2026 call). That process was interrupted mid-flow: the shortlist was never completed, and on 26 May 2026 Tharun disclosed a new internal IT-governance team and cross-department approval workflow now gates the decision beyond the sourcing team, with the project 'implemented throughout the company' rather than owned solely by sourcing.

decision_criteria.narrative: Voiced criteria are concrete: CLM extraction accuracy validated against DuBois's own contract samples (Chris Klein and Tharun, 11 Mar 2026), real-time bidirectional integration with D365 and Coupa without replacing either (3 Dec 2025), and minimal manual effort/ease of use for the legal team (Chris Klein, 11 Mar 2026: 'my dream software would be... two clicks and I'm done'). None of these has been formally confirmed because the sandbox proof and integration assessment that would validate them remain outstanding.

north_star_verdict.headline: Deal cooled for 42 days after the buyer explicitly paused the evaluation to a Q3 2026 restart; the current 30 Jul 2026 close date is not credible.

flags: Economic buyer Jackie Zhang (VP Procurement, Decision Maker role) has been absent since mid-April (~11 weeks) and did not attend any Q2 calls (Feb AI call, March CLM demo, May check-in). Tharun drives the evaluation but defers commercial and budget decisions to the relevant stakeholder the project restarts in Q3 Jackie must re-engage and validate the business case, or this stays a team-level productivity project that can be paused again. Recommended move: book an exec connect with Jackie in Q3 to present a quantified ROI tied to the $300–400M spend base and position Zycus as her strategic pick, not just Tharun's preference.

No quantified ROI or business case. DuBois articulated pain (fragmented CLM, manual sourcing, no AI analytics) but never quantified savings, cost reduction, or cycle-time improvement across the $300–400M spend base managed by 9–10 people. Zycus shared pricing ($85K+ ballpark, detailed quote 30 Jan) but DuBois never validated value vs cost. Tharun stated on 26 May a new IT governance approval workflow is now in place and the project must pass a cross-department budget gate. Without a board-ready ROI this will not clear that gate. Recommended move: run an ROI workshop with Tharun + Jackie in Q3, quantify savings on contract cycle-time, indirect-spend consolidation, and AI-driven negotiation, and tie it to their spend base to build the business case for the IT approval workflow.

Multi-vendor bake-off is frozen with no Zycus differentiation anchored. Tharun is evaluating ~10 vendors with a plan to shortlist to top 3, but the process paused in April before any finalist was named. Coupa (incumbent for onboarding + invoicing) is the embedded threat if they counter-propose a CLM or sourcing expansion. Zycus has not been positioned as the front-runner and has no unique wedge anchored in the buyer's mind beyond 'we saw good demos'. Recommended move: when the deal restarts, lead with the AI differentiation story (conversational analytics, autonomous negotiation, predictive sourcing) as the strategic layer above Coupa's transactional plumbing, prove seamless D365 + Coupa integration, and make the recap demo an ROI-led business case, not a feature tour, to own the top spot in the shortlist.

Protect CLM's position in scope as DuBois's management pivots priority to the sourcing board -- reinforce the CLM/AI differentiation (NegoGenie, obligation extraction) shown on 11 Mar 2026 before the sourcing-only narrative hardens further.

Champion Tharun is working-level only; without looping Jackie Zhang (VP Procurement, economic buyer) back in, budget approval through DuBois's new cross-department gate stalls regardless of feature fit.

competitive_position.summary: Coupa is the single strongest current factor: it is deeply embedded in DuBois's invoicing and supplier-onboarding flow and DuBois has stated no plan to replace it, making it both a co-existence anchor and the natural low-switching-cost alternative if DuBois narrows scope. win_position.text: The stated economic buyer, Jackie Zhang (VP Procurement), has never appeared on a recorded call despite repeated scheduling attempts between 4 Mar and 13 Apr 2026.

No quantified ROI or business case has been produced against the buyer's $300-400M spend base across more than seven months of engagement.

Coupa is a deeply embedded incumbent the buyer says it will not replace, and Zycus's own consultant suggested on 26 Feb 2026 that keeping supplier onboarding in Coupa might be the 'natural choice' — a self-inflicted competitive opening.

The buyer's own stated restart window ('end of Q2') passed on 30 Jun 2026 without a reconnect, and the recap demo the buyer asked for on 26 May 2026 remains undelivered.

Ryan Anderson's promised IT integration assessment for D365 + Coupa sync, first flagged as a dependency on 26 Feb 2026, still has not been completed or reported back.

I'll be representing the sourcing team' — 26 May 2026), meaning DuBois-side commitment currently sits below the level that can approve spend.

deltas.to: No quantified ROI or business case. DuBois articulated pain (fragmented CLM, manual sourcing, no AI analytics) but never quantified savings, cost reduction, or
deltas.subject: No quantified ROI or business case. DuBois articulated pain (fragmented CLM, manual sourcing, no AI analytics) but never quantified savings, cost reduction, or cycle-time improvement across the $300–400M spend base managed by 9–10 people. Zycus shared pricing ($85K+ ballpark, detailed quote 30 Jan) but DuBois never validated value vs cost. Tharun stated on 26 May a new IT governance approval workflow is now in place and the project must pass a cross-department budget gate. Without a board-ready ROI this will not clear that gate. Recommended move: run an ROI workshop with Tharun + Jackie in Q3, quantify savings on contract cycle-time, indirect-spend consolidation, and AI-driven negotiation, and tie it to their spend base to build the business case for the IT approval workflow.

deltas.to: Multi-vendor bake-off is frozen with no Zycus differentiation anchored. Tharun is evaluating ~10 vendors with a plan to shortlist to top 3, but the process paus
deltas.subject: Multi-vendor bake-off is frozen with no Zycus differentiation anchored. Tharun is evaluating ~10 vendors with a plan to shortlist to top 3, but the process paused in April before any finalist was named. Coupa (incumbent for onboarding + invoicing) is the embedded threat if they counter-propose a CLM or sourcing expansion. Zycus has not been positioned as the front-runner and has no unique wedge anchored in the buyer's mind beyond 'we saw good demos'. Recommended move: when the deal restarts, lead with the AI differentiation story (conversational analytics, autonomous negotiation, predictive sourcing) as the strategic layer above Coupa's transactional plumbing, prove seamless D365 + Coupa integration, and make the recap demo an ROI-led business case, not a feature tour, to own the top spot in the shortlist.
